original_hypothesis.title: setMarketplaceSettingAddress allows admin to redirect protocol to malicious settings contract
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The admin can call setMarketplaceSettingAddress to point the marketplace to an entirely new MarketplaceSetting contract with arbitrary fee rates (up to 50% buyer + 50% seller = 100% extraction), a different feeCollector (attacker wallet), a different vestingManager, and a different whitelistDeployer. This effectively redirects all marketplace fee revenue and can compromise the entire trading flow.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: There is no timelock, no multi-sig requirement, and no event-based warning period. The change is immediate and affects all subsequent transactions. While this requires admin key access, the single-admin model with instant effect creates a catastrophic blast radius.

proof_of_signal.pattern: setMarketplaceSettingAddress replaces entire settings contract with no timelock, validation of contract interface, or delay

preconditions: Admin key is compromised or admin acts maliciously
Attacker deploys a malicious MarketplaceSetting contract

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: New settings contract can return any fee rates, any feeCollector address, any vestingManager
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Instant redirect of all marketplace operations to attacker-controlled infrastructure
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: 100% of all subsequent trade volumes can be extracted as fees to attacker's feeCollector

economic_questions: What total trade volume could be affected before users notice?
Can the malicious settings contract also manipulate the freeze status?
Does redirecting vestingManager break existing listings?
validation_requirements: Deploy malicious settings contract, call setMarketplaceSettingAddress, verify fee redirection
Test that existing listings use the new settings for purchases
Verify no validation on the new settings contract interface

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Gas to deploy malicious contract and call setter
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Up to 100% of all future marketplace trade volume
preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Until detected and admin key recovered

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: When marketplace has high active listing volume

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Up to 100% of trade value extracted as fees
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All marketplace TVL and future trade volume

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Review what interfaces the marketplace reads from settings
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Verify malicious settings can return arbitrary values for all fields

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
